While Anugrah Tambang Sejahtera held an inauguration event in 2019, there is no evidence that construction was completed or operations began. PT Anugrah Tambang Sejahtera was acquired by Silkroad Nickel in 2021, and noted plans to expedite development of an RKEF smelter in Sulawesi. According to a court case involving PT. Anugerah Netzero Nikel, PT Teknik Alum Service, and Hong Kah Ing (the President Director of of PT Anugerah Netzero Nikel and President Commissioner of PT Teknik Alum Service), PT Anugerah Tambang Sejahtera was rebranded PT Anugerah Netzero Nikel. PT Anugrah Netzero Nickel was further rebranded to PT Anugrah Neo Energy Materials in 2023. The PT Anugrah Neo Energy Materials makes no mention of a functioning RKEF or blast furnace nickel smelter as one of their projects.

The input capacity of Ni ore at a 1.65% grade is a total of 1,440,000 tons per year for 12 furnaces. The basic output assumption for each furnace is that it will produce NPI is 8-10%, which is 12,000 tons per year, which means that if 12 furnaces are operational, the nickel produced will be 144,000 tons per year. Status 2025 Notes:
While this project was initially inaugurated by PT Vale Indonesia as an RKEF facility located in Sambalagi, Morowali, it appears the project plan has been changed to an HPAL. According to PT Vale Indonesia's Q2 2025 Presentation, construction progress at the HPAL was 6.5%, with earthwork construction starting. The project has undergone several ownership changes, with GEM becoming the majority owner of PT Bahodopi Nickel Smelting Indonesia in March 2025 and Danatara joining as a strategic investor in August 2025. PT Vale Indonesia maintains 30% ownership.

Source 12, Ferroalloynet, August 28, 2025: Danantara, Gem Ltd., and Vale Indonesia (INCO) Partner in Nickel Smelter Project

Vale Indonesia has signed a strategic collaboration with GEM Co., Ltd. regarding the construction of a nickel smelter facility with HPAL technology in Central Sulawesi in November 2025. Muhammad Ishaq, President Director of PT Ifishdeco Tbk, has finally spoken out in response to negative accusations, including claims that the company lied to the public and that the smelter they built is stalled.

He stated that the construction of the PT Ifishdeco smelter, which was built in 2014, focused on the production of Nickel Pig Iron or NPI.
"We built the smelter in 2014 with PT PP (Persero) Tbk, purely to support the government's downstreaming program. It wasn't to pursue export quotas, as export relaxation was only issued in 2017," Ishaq explained in a statement received by Lajur.co on Friday (July 18, 2025). He showed several videos of production activities and export shipments of NPI products as evidence that the BSI smelter had been operating normally.
However, BSI's journey wasn't smooth sailing. The blast furnace technology it used proved uneconomical. BSI had to import coke from China, whose costs continued to soar, accounting for 40% of total production costs. As a result, the company continued to incur losses.
Our investment in BSI reached Rp 350 billion. But it turns out it's not just us; other companies using blast furnace technology have also stopped operating," he said.
To support downstream processing, BSI had planned to build a new smelter with Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace (RKEF) technology. The project even partnered with PLN to build a substation. However, these plans were shelved due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
"Experts from China who were supposed to transfer knowledge to local workers couldn't come. We prioritize public safety," said Ishaq.
Now, BSI is not alone. Muhammad Ishaq revealed that recently, RKEF smelters have also begun to face significant pressure. Some have even begun closing and laying off employees due to high operational costs.

According to BloombergTechnoz.com, Indonesia's RKEF-based nickel smelter industry is under severe pressure. Several major players have partially or completely halted production since the beginning of this year due to shrinking profit margins, primarily due to declining demand for stainless steel from China and rising production costs.
Djoko Widajatno, a member of the Advisory Board of the Indonesian Miners Association (APNI), stated that four companies have halted production. These include PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI), PT Indonesia Tsingshan Stainless Steel (ITSS), both located in the IMIP area in Central Sulawesi. The other companies are PT Virtue Dragon Nickel Industry (VDNI), and PT Huadi Nickel Alloy Indonesia (HNAI), both based in Konawe Regency, Southeast Sulawesi.
Despite facing challenges in the smelter sector, PT Ifishdeco Tbk continues to operate in Ngapaaha Village, Tinanggea District, South Konawe Regency (Konsel). Over its 17-year history, the company has achieved numerous achievements.
In 2021, Ifishdeco received an award from the Kendari Tax Office (KPP) as the taxpayer with the highest tax payments in the mining and quarrying sector. Environmentally, Ifishdeco earned the Blue Proper rating from the Ministry of Environment and Forestry for two consecutive years, for the 2022–2023 and 2023–2024 assessment periods."

"PT Ceria Nugraha Indotama's (Ceria Group) nickel smelter has finally started producing green nickel and will continue construction of the smelter with a capacity of 252,800 tons per year. The Red and White Smelter owned by PT Ceria, located in Wolo, Kolaka Regency, has succeeded in producing ferronickel for the first time, which is a major commitment towards a sustainable industry based on Environmental, Social, and Governance ( ESG ).
Ceria Group CEO Derian Sakmiwata said that the monumental achievement with the smelter's Project Commercial Operation Date (PCOD) target which has been running on time, strengthens Indonesia's position as a pioneer in the development of an ESG-based green nickel industry...
"At this special moment, Smelter Merah Putih has successfully produced its first ferronickel. Through technological innovation, PT Ceria ensures that the ferronickel produced is not only of high quality, but also carries a mission of sustainability," he explained, quoted Monday (4/28/2025).
Derian emphasized that this initial production is just the beginning. PT Ceria is ready to continue the construction of RKEF Line 2, Line 3, and Line 4, targeting a total production capacity of 252,800 tons of ferronickel per year.

General Manager of RKEF Operation Readiness PT Ceria, Roimon Barus explained that the Merah Putih Ceria smelter adopted Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace (RKEF) technology with a capacity of 72 MVA to produce 63,200 tons of ferronickel per year or around 13,900 tons of nickel metal.
This smelter also uses a Rectangular Electric Furnace, a rectangular furnace design that is able to retain heat longer, increase energy efficiency, and significantly reduce exhaust emissions.
"All production processes are supported by green energy from PLN UID Sulselrabar which is certified with Renewable Energy Certificate [REC], making the Merah Putih Smelter one of the nickel industrial facilities with the lowest carbon footprint in Indonesia," he explained. Roimon continued, PT Ceria is not only talking about production, but also about the green transition. Source 4, Growth Java Industry, 2025: "T. Growth Java Industry (GJI) was established in 2006 as the expansion of the Growth Steel Group under the leadership of Chairman Mr. Fadjar Suhendra, meant to reach broader domestic market of steel and iron, especially Central to East of Indonesia. The company's production facility is located in Cilegon, Banten. As in January 2014, the Indonesian government enacted new mining regulations requiring all kinds of mineral ores to be processed into higher value-added products prior to export. It was then GJI shifted its focus to the production of Nickel Pig Iron (NPI) and Ferro Nickel (FeNi), becoming the first new smelter in the country to begin operations under this regulation.
Since its establishment, the company has been dedicated to enhancing the value of Indonesia's mineral resources into high-value products, extracting nickel ore into NPI and FeNi with a nickel content up to 23%. Having production capacity of 10,000 tons NPI/FeNi per month signifies contribution to the growth of Indonesia's mineral processing industry.
Moving forward, GJI is expanding its operations to more variety products including production of Ferrosilicon (FeSi) with installed capacity up to 7,000 tons FeSi per month with silicon content minimum 75%. The firing started by end of Q3-2025. GJI has implemented and been certified with ISO 9001:2015 by ISC Tokyo, reinforcing its commitment to quality management and continuous improvement...
Supporting its operations, the company has established wide range of the following facilities:
Blast Furnace, capacity 450 m³, capable to produce BPI 500,000 mtpa.
Electric Furnace Plant, 2 × 42MVA each, capable to produce FeSi 100,000 mtpa.
Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace (RKEF) Plant, 2 × 16.5MVA, capable to produce NPI 100,000 mtpa.
Rotary Kiln Electric Furnace (RKEF) Plant, 2 × 42MVA, capable to produce FeNi 160,000 mtpa.
Coke Oven Plant with production capacity 300,000 mtpa to support iron making process in Blast Furnace.
Slag Grinding Plant; GBFS into GGBFS capacity 240,000 mtpa.
Silica Fume capacity 8,000 mtpa.
Crushing Plant capacity 1,000,000 mtpa.
Products:
Nickel Pig Iron (Ni: 8–12%) & Ferro Nickel (Ni >12%) : Primarily used as a raw material in stainless steel production, providing an efficient and cost-effective nickel source for alloying.
Ferro Silicon (Si: min. 75%) : Commonly used as a deoxidizer and alloying agent in steelmaking, enhancing strength and quality in various steel grades.
Dry Slag for Aggregate : A by-product of nickel pig iron production, used as a substitute for natural aggregates in construction, promoting sustainable building practices.
Silica Fume : A by-product of ferrosilicon production, composed of amorphous silicon dioxide, used as a concrete admixture to improve strength, durability, and chemical resistance."

Source 7, Bloomberg Technoz (on Danantara Monitor), August 12, 2025: "The Indonesian Nickel Miners Association (APNI) said Daya Anagata Nusantara Investment Management Agency (BPI Danantara) has the opportunity to form a consortium with a Chinese company to acquire PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry (GNI)’s nickel smelter. 
APNI Mining Advisory Board Member Djoko Widajatno said the news on Danantara acquiring GNI smelter is still in development. He said Danantara is still waiting for a green light from the authorities and the due diligence process.
“This process is currently in evaluation and due diligence phase, with plans to collaborate with MIND ID, the parent company of a state-owned mining company, as the main partner in the acquisition,” Djoko said when contacted on Tuesday (August 12, 2025).
Djoko also believes there is a possibility that Danantara will collaborate with a Chinese company, as the GNI acquisition is expected to be quite expensive. However, he has not received any updates on funding negotiations from the financial consortium. “For the time being, that’s all [the information] we had; a consortium from China to collaborate with Danantara is a possibility,” Djoko said. According to Djoko, Danantara will prepare a preliminary funding around US$20 billion to acquire GNI. Besides coming from Danantara, Djoko said, the funding will come from a US$60 million syndicated loan to support a mid-term liquidity. “As of now, PT GNI remains under Jiangsu Delong Nickel Industry Co., a Chinese parent company,” he said. A Danantara representative did not give response when asked for confirmation.
Production Halt
Separately, Djoko explained that PT GNI has more than 20 lines of nickel pig iron production (NPI) for stainless steel raw material. Still, almost all the production lines have been offline since early 2024 and only “some lines are still running”.
“It is estimated that more than 15 production lines [of GNI] have been stopped, potentially close to a total shutdown,” Djoko said when contacted in July.
According to Director General of Metal, Machine, Transportation, and Electronics  Industries at the Ministry of Industry Setia Diarta, the subsidiary of Chinese conglomerate Jiangsu Delong is still waiting to have a scheduled meeting with its creditors to get a fresh funding. “GNI is currently waiting for its creditors’ meeting schedule which could be in August. That the update we received,” Setia told Bloomberg Technoz when asked for confirmation recently.
Until the funding process with the new investor is complete, Setia emphasized that PT GNI remains a subsidiary of Jiangsu Delong, which has been facing financial issues in its home country of China since last year. BPI Danantara Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Rosan Roeslani previously confirmed that he is open to acquiring the PT GNI smelter, which is part of the National Strategic Project (PSN) for nickel downstreaming. The investment plan is currently under review.
Rosan stated that Danantara is indeed considering investing in similar downstream projects. If the smelter owned by the Jiangsu Delong subsidiary meets the investment criteria, he emphasized, Danantara could invest in PT GNI.
“There are several projects in the pipeline that we are considering. We will see which ones are feasible and good, so we will review them all,” Rosan said at the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources on Tuesday (July 22, 2025).
The GNI smelter is included in the National National Project (PSN) based on Coordinating Minister for Economic Affairs Regulation Number 7 of 2021, and is part of a joint project with PT Virtue Dragon Nickel Industry (VDNI), a subsidiary of Jiangsu Delong. PT GNI’s operations are located in Bunta Village, Petasia Timur District, North Morowali Regency, Central Sulawesi. Using pyrometallurgical technology, also known as rotary kiln electric furnace (RKEF), the GNI smelter has a production capacity of 1.9 million NPI per year.
In addition, the company produces ferronickel, which is then processed into raw materials used in stainless steel and the nickel alloy industry..."

1. A Worker Burned on a Crane
In late December 2022, a fire broke out at one of GNI's smelters . Tragically, a female worker named Nirwana died after being trapped in a nearby crane . The incident also killed Nirwana, a colleague named I Made Defri.
2. Clashes between Local and Foreign Workers
Shortly after, in early January 2023, a clash broke out between local Indonesian workers and foreign workers at the Indonesian National Workers' Union (DI GNI). The clash also left two people dead and nine others injured.
It is known that one of the causes of the clash was triggered by workers' demands which had not been responded to by the company.
3. Fire Spray 
Then, in June 2023, another incident occurred at PT GNI's site. The accident occurred while workers were installing a filter screen (grinding division) at GNI's smelter 1.
Tragically, a sudden burst of fire erupted and engulfed seven workers. One worker, Ferdi, was pronounced dead, and six others were injured.
4. Furnace Flame Burst
Three months later, another incident occurred at the GNI smelter furnace. On September 28, 2023, one of the smelter furnaces on the factory grounds erupted in flames, killing one worker.
The fire was triggered by slag or nickel waste used in processing in furnace number 7 of smelter 1. At the time of the incident, the newly taken slag was still hot and exploded when cooled with water.
5. End of Year Fires
Another accident occurred on Thursday (December 28, 2023), when the GNI smelter in North Morowali caught fire again. The blaze reportedly started from welding sparks in an area near the factory. According to police reports, no casualties were reported in the fire."

Source 4, Nikel.co,  August 12, 2024: " Another unfortunate incident has occurred at a nickel smelter in Indonesia. This time, it occurred at PT Lestari Smelter Indonesia (LSI), located in the PT Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park (IMIP) area in Bahodopi District, Morowali, Central Sulawesi.
According to detik.com, the leak began during the melting process of hot molten metal on Friday (August 9, 2024). The smelter furnace leaked, causing hot molten metal to spill out and hit the floor. 
The molten lava overflowed and caused a massive fire. The smelter has been temporarily closed due to the incident.
"During the melting process, when tapping was about to begin, some liquid came out. This liquid is hot, reaching a temperature of 1,400 degrees," said PT IMIP Media Relations Head Dedy Kurniawan, quoted on Monday (August 10, 2024).
Dedy explained the chronology of events leading up to the furnace leak. Abrasion of the furnace fluid suddenly occurred during the melting process.
"The abrasion of the furnace fluid, which we usually call molten metal, spilled onto the smelter floor," he said.
According to him, the company's safety officers quickly carried out cooling and other workers were immediately evacuated to a safe place.
"Then we tried to cool it by spraying water, and after 45 minutes it was cold," explained Dedy.
However, Dedy emphasized that the incident was not a fire, as the smelter was made of steel and concrete.
"The smelter here only uses two materials: steel and concrete, so saying it was a fire is not quite accurate," he added.
He confirmed there were no fatalities or injuries in the incident. However, his team temporarily closed the PT LSI smelter for investigation.
"There were no casualties, neither injuries nor deaths, operations were suspended for an indefinite period," he said.

Source 1, Pyrometallurgy.co.za, 2009: “In 1977, Inco Limited (today Vale Inco) commissioned two nickel laterite operations, one in Sorowako, Indonesia and the other one in Lake Izabal, Guatemala. Both ore processing plants used the standard drying-reduction kiln-electric furnace route used by ferronickel producers. However, the product at the two Inco locations was a low-iron nickel matte. To achieve this goal, a controlled amount of sulfur was added to the discharge of the reduction kiln to generate in turn sulfur-deficient nickel-iron matte in the electric furnace. This material was converted to low-iron nickel matte that was granulated and shipped to overseas facilities where it was processed to market products. This pyrometallurgical route is still used in the now much larger Sorowako operation.”

Operational nickel smelter currently undergoing a lawsuit for environmental damage and under scrutiny for failure to pay taxes. Workers have also protested against unfair treatment.

Source 5, Antara Sultra, July 11, 2025: "Commission XII of the Regional People's Representative Council (DPR) of the Republic of Indonesia (RI) highlighted the tax arrears of the companies PT Virtue Dragonc Nickel Industry (VDNI) and PT Obsidian Stainless Steel (OSS) in Morosi District, Konawe Regency, Southeast Sulawesi Province (Sultra). Member of Commission XII of the Indonesian House of Representatives, Rocky Candra, when met in Kendari, Friday, said that based on the results of the presentation by the Governor of Southeast Sulawesi Andi Sumangerukka, PT VDNI and OSS had not paid their obligations regarding taxes for Regional Original Income or PAD for the past 4 to 5 years. "For the past four or five years, they have not paid their obligations related to surface water tax, motor vehicle tax, and fuel tax," said Rocky Candra.
He urged PT VDNI and PT OSS to immediately fulfill their obligations regarding the Rp5.6 billion surface water tax, as it significantly impacts the development and welfare of the Southeast Sulawesi region. "I ask Virtue Dragon to immediately pay its obligations to the Southeast Sulawesi regional government," he said. He stated that the results of the review by Commission XII of the Indonesian House of Representatives also found that there was a lawsuit in court filed against PT VDNI and OSS by the Regional Government, which is currently still ongoing. "We'll see what the outcome of this legal process will be. Of course, once a legal decision is made, the VDNI and OSS must comply," said Rocky Candra.
Rocky also expressed his disappointment over the answers and responses from the management of PT VDNI and PT OSS which he considered very unsatisfactory regarding their visit to Southeast Sulawesi. "So, based on the information we've gathered, and the information provided by Virtue and OSS management, we've expressed a great deal of dissatisfaction, and we will be bringing this issue to the Minerba Working Committee at Commission XII," explained Rocky Candra."

Source 4, Kendari Info, June 23, 2025: "Residents in Morosi District, Konawe Regency, Southeast Sulawesi (Sultra), reported directly the impact of the activities of two smelter companies, PT Virtue Dragon Nickel Industry ( VDNI ) and PT Obsidian Stainless Steel (OSS), to the panel of judges at the Unaaha District Court (PN) when they conducted a local inspection (PS), Thursday (19/6/2025). Residents also pointed out ponds covered in sediment, rivers polluted by waste, and limited access to clean water due to the operations of PT VDNI and OSS. These complaints and facts on the ground are the basis of the lawsuit filed by residents affected by the PT VDNI and OSS industries at the Unaaha District Court under case number 28/Pdt.Sus-LH/2024/PN Unh. Andi Rahman, Director of the Indonesian Forum for the Environment (Walhi) Southeast Sulawesi, who also advocates for affected residents, said the panel of judges' on-site inspection is an important step in demonstrating that the environmental issues caused by PT VDNI and OSS's operations are not just talk. The impacts of the two smelter companies' activities are a reality, directly harming residents.
"The damage to the ponds and the declining environmental quality have caused significant economic losses for the farmers. Residents also continue to complain of health problems, such as shortness of breath, skin irritation, and other respiratory issues due to dust and emissions from the captive coal-fired power plant and massive industrial activity," Andi said in a written statement to Kendariinfo on Thursday (June 19). The on-site inspection by the Unaaha District Court panel of judges is part of the evidentiary process in the unlawful act lawsuit filed on the grounds of environmental damage, violation of the right to health, and economic losses to residents due to pollution from the captive coal-fired power plant and nickel production activities. According to Andi, the case filed with the Unaaha District Court sets an important precedent for environmental law enforcement in Southeast Sulawesi. "We hope this legal process can provide momentum to stop reckless industrialization practices that disregard public safety," he concluded."

Source 3, China Labor Watch, February 27, 2025: "In December 2020, protesting VDNI workers set parts of the plant ablaze after the firm refused their demands to put employees who had been there for more than three years on the permanent payroll and give a pay rise to those who had been there for one year."

Source 4, Taipei Times, August 7, 2025: "Taipei-based conglomerate Walsin Lihwa Corp. was accused yesterday of causing severe pollution and human rights violations at its factories in Indonesia by local environmental groups and workers. Indonesian NGOs — the Indonesian Forum for the Environment (WALHI) and Action for Ecology and People's Emancipation (AEER) — along with Taiwan's Environmental Rights Foundation (ERF), held a press conference at the Legislative Yuan, urging the government to address transnational violations and tighten oversight of overseas investments. Johan Sappara, a worker at one of the company's Indonesian factories, said employees were given only surgical masks to protect against toxic air and coal dust. He added that occupational safety was poorly managed, with procedures that were poorly implemented for fall-related accidents.
Sappara also said the factory had only one foul-smelling lavatory for its 1,000 workers and that the resting area lacked proper ventilation.
In a statement released Tuesday, ERF said the company operates nickel smelters and a coal-fired power plant in Indonesia's Morowali Bay Industrial Park and Weda Bay Industrial Park.
...In a statement released Tuesday, Walsin Lihwa said it had addressed the accusations and expressed regret over what it called "inaccurate" information from environmental groups.
The company said its power plants meet all environmental regulations and that its mining suppliers are legally approved by the Indonesian government. It also claimed it has no partnership with the suppliers named by the groups. Regarding a recent workplace accident, the company said it responded promptly and that an Indonesian government investigation had been completed in line with insurance and compensation regulations. It added that it provided compensation well above the legal requirement as a gesture of care."

Source 4, Media Alkhairaat, May 26, 2025: "A number of residents of Lele Village, Bahodopi District, Morowali Regency, Central Sulawesi, have blocked the farm road used by PT Wanxiang Nickel Indonesia (WNI) for the past three years. The blockade, which began on May 6, 2025, was a form of protest against the company's alleged default regarding payments for land owned by residents. Residents believe that PT WNI has broken its promise regarding the agreement that was reached jointly in a number of meetings mediated by the village government and witnessed by security forces. Until now, compensation has not been paid according to the agreement for land belonging to a number of residents that was used for company activities.
"Actually, we've been patient. The company once agreed to pay Rp 100,000 per meter, but we haven't paid yet," said Wati, one of the landowners, when met at the blockade site on Monday (May 26). Wati said that the road that was blocked was a farm road that had been used by residents long before the company arrived, not a road belonging to the company.
He also regretted the company's move to report residents to the police on charges of obstructing foreign investment.
"If it's a company road and it's been paid for, why didn't we know about it? Why weren't we involved? This is a farm road, not a company road," he added. Residents stated that they would not lift the blockade until the company provided clarity and completed payment for the land. They also hoped the police would be fair in handling the matter. "We are ready to comply with the police summons, but we also demand that the company be investigated because they are the ones who broke their promises. Since the company arrived, our gardens have been flooded every rainy season because the river was dammed," Wati said, sounding irritated.
Meanwhile, a spokesperson for PT WNI, when confirmed via WhatsApp message, stated that the company had completely handed over the matter to law enforcement officials.
"The company management has conveyed that the issue of residents blocking roads and obstructing foreign investment has been handed over to the police. Thank you," he wrote briefly.
Field observations indicate that PT WNI has dammed the Dampala River using elephant rocks. The river's water is used for the company's needs, and there is no mechanism for opening and closing the dam. As a result, during heavy rains, residents' agricultural land is often inundated."

About PTWANXIANG NICKEL INDONESIA
PT. WANXIANG NICKEL INDONESIA is located in Morowali, Central Sulawesi, Indonesia, with 890 hectares areas, surrounded by mountains on three sides. The company is planned to be divided into four areas in the east, west, south and north: the east area is the maritime terminal logistics area, the south area is planned as the hydrometallurgy area, the west area is the future development area, and the north area is the first and second phase nickel pig iron (ferronickel) smelting and power supply areas.

Bambang revealed that his party received a report that the mining company carrying out mining activities in Kolaka, Southeast Sulawesi, was suspected of exporting by using production from outside its mine. "He (Toshida) must clarify everything our supervisors discovered. I received information from outside that he didn't take it from his own mine," Bambang told Bisnis on Thursday (August 16, 2018).
Then another finding, namely Toshida was reported in its export activities allegedly using other ports that did not comply with the permits it had. "After we checked, it turns out there are actually two ports there. So both can be used, but only one was reported," said Bambang. Therefore, Bambang requested that Toshida immediately amend and update its report. The company had failed to report its production and export activities for three consecutive years. These reports should be submitted monthly. In this regard, the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources has issued a final warning to Toshida. Bambang stated that his ministry has given Toshida 60 days from the date of the last warning letter to submit its report and provide clarification on the field findings. The last warning letter was issued last July. If Toshida does not submit its latest report within the given time limit, the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources will likely temporarily suspend the company's export permit. Thosida Indonesia, through PT Asia Mining Minerals, is building a smelter in Kolaka, Southeast Sulawesi. Physical progress has only reached 2.8%. Meanwhile, in the six-monthly evaluation plan, the company's smelter's processing and refining facility, or smelter, is targeted to reach 8.41%. Source 2, Detik Sultra, July 9, 2019: "The sale of iron for the construction of the PT. Surya Saga Utama (SSU) smelter in Lampangi Jaya, Tedubara Village, North Kabaena District, Bombana Regency, has received serious attention from the NGO Movement for Law and Regional Policy Observers (LSM-Gerhana) of Bombana Regency.This was conveyed by Mayon Susanto Husin, Executive Director of the LSM-Gerhana, to the media on Monday (July 8, 2019). He stated that the sale of the company's assets, which entered Indonesia under tax-free facilities, was not misused, especially since the sale was carried out by the kilo before the smelter construction materials were used.
"This smelter equipment entered Indonesia with tax-free access. The clear purpose was to build a smelter plant. Therefore, if the construction is cancelled, it cannot simply be sold as scrap metal. This is what the company doesn't understand," said the activist who focuses on environmental issues and community advocacy.
Mayon Susanto continued, saying that the state suffered significant losses due to this incident. Therefore, the institution he leads is requesting that law enforcement, specifically the police and customs, take action to prevent further state losses.
"We hope law enforcement officials will intervene and monitor the iron smelter sales process, especially since this isn't just happening now. It's suspected that the sales have been carried out repeatedly, even amounting to thousands of tons. Calculate the state's losses," he explained.
When asked for confirmation, PT. SSU Director Kasra J. Munara confirmed the sale of smelter equipment by an internal party acting on behalf of an investor through a power of attorney from the board of directors.
However, Kasra denied allegations that he had condoned the alleged illegal activity. He even admitted that he had tried to prevent it because he knew it was inconsistent with the company's plan to restart the smelter.
"I have reminded those claiming to be company directors not to sell the smelter's iron, as this is against regulations, and it is true that this smelter equipment is imported with tax exemption," said the former Bombana Regent Candidate.
When asked about the news of the change in PT. SSU's board of directors, Kasra explained that he had seen the letter circulating from several people, even circulating in the KNPI Bombana WhatsApp group. However, Kasra felt there were several oddities in the letter.
"There could be elements of document forgery in the letter, and I have reported this to the authorities. I suspect there are internal parties who want to get rid of me because I obstructed the sale of iron in violation of the regulations," he concluded.
For your information, before carrying out mass layoffs in November 2018, PT. SSU promised to make improvements by upgrading its technology so it could resume operations, and eventually rehire the laid-off employees. However, to date, there are no signs that PT SSU will restart its smelter. On the contrary, some of the smelter's materials and equipment have even been sold by the kilogram. The PT. SSU smelter was once a project of pride in Southeast Sulawesi, particularly in Bombana, as it was expected to generate tens of trillions of rupiah in investment."

Source 2, Betahita, September 28, 2022: "...Meanwhile, Sagea Village, North Weda, Central Halmahera, is gripped by anxiety about suffering a similar fate. Adlun Fiqri, a young man from Sagea, said his livelihood is now threatened by the planned mining activities of PT First Pacific Mining near his village and another village, Kiya.
According to Adlun, the company's 2,000-hectare nickel mining concession is located in the Bokimoruru karst area. Meanwhile, the planned factory development site for PT First Pacific Mining is located on the Sageyan River and Lake Legaelol, which also fall within the karst ecosystem. It is also close to residential areas.
Adlun, who was once active in the North Maluku Indigenous Peoples' Alliance of the Archipelago (AMAN), believes the company's entry failed to follow proper procedures. Furthermore, he has received information that the company is planning to build a smelter. However, he believes the company has been non-transparent regarding permitting information and environmental impact analysis (AMDAL). "One of them is that the company received a permit from the district in 2012, which was then renewed in 2018. The AMDAL provisions should have included an addendum. We never even saw any public consultation," Adlun said.
Residents also received insufficient information about the company's goals and activities. They were only informed about the company's land prices. Yet, most of them own land licensed to PT First Pacific Mining, a Chinese company. "We knew some of it, but this information wasn't widely disseminated. On the one hand, residents who would be affected have the right to express their acceptance or rejection. But suddenly, the permit was issued," Adlun explained.
Adlun believes this fails to meet Free, Prior, and Informed Consent (FPIC) requirements. This refers to the right of indigenous or local communities to give or withhold consent to any project that has the potential to impact land, livelihoods, and the environment. Adlun worries that the company's activities in these locations will negatively impact his village. The river is a source of water for drinking, bathing, and washing clothes. The karst area also functions as a water catchment area and stores water reserves. The utilization of the Bokimoruru Cave and Legaelol Lake karst tourism also brings economic benefits to Sagea and Kiya Villages...
It's known that the PT First Pacific Mining industrial area will occupy 1,000 hectares of land. According to Adlun, it will be only about 500 meters from the Sagea River and Lake Legaelol.
Adlun also accused the company of causing polarization within the community. Some residents have agreed to and accepted compensation from the company. However, many others have refused to relinquish their land.  "We've seen concrete evidence that mining is a dirty industry with environmental damage. That's why the Save the Village Movement exists," said Adlun.
Together with youth from Kiya Village, residents established the Sagea-Kiya Village Rescue Coalition (SEKA) at the end of 2014. However, it became active again this year when the community heard information that PT First Pacific Mining would start operations. This movement continues to campaign about the potential damage that mining companies could cause if they continue their operations. On one occasion, after the protest, residents also met with the company at the Central Halmahera Regent's office.
PT First Pacific Mining's industrial estate plans to build a 160,000-ton nickel ore processing plant. The company will also build a 750 MW coal-fired power plant and a 50,000-ton pier. The company received a permit from the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources in 2018, valid until 2032. The ministry's map shows PT First Pacific Mining's production operations located behind the village. The concession covers 2,080 hectares. The concession was previously managed by PT Zong Hai. Quoted from Mongabay Indonesia , the company also promised to build houses of worship, roads, and develop ecotourism after the factory and smelter construction was completed.  According to Adlun, this is not the wish of the residents of Sagea and Kiya villages. Instead, the coalition is urging PT First Pacific Mining to cease all activities in the villages. They are also urging the district and provincial governments to review the company's permits. In addition, the government is also being urged to immediately issue a policy to protect the karst area in Sagea Village.

Source 4, Indonesia Business Post, February 24, 2025: "A worker at PT Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park (IMIP), a nickel smelter industrial complex in Morowali, Southeast Sulawesi has died after a heavy object crushed his head at the smelter of Nickel Pig Iron (NPI) producer PT Ocean Sky Metal Industry.
Executive chairman of the Morowali Industrial Workers' Union-Confederation of Indonesian Workers' Unions (SPIM-KPBI), Komang Jordi Segara, told Indonesia Business Post that on February 16, 2025, Marjan Daud, a worker at PT Ocean Sky Metal Industry, was cleaning hardened HB (solidified matte liquid) from the londer (liquid flow channel) at about 9.30 a.m. local time. While pushing the HB at the edge of the londer with his hands, his gloves got caught in the HB, which weighed around 150 kg, and the heavy object crushed the right side of his head.
Komang Jordi condemned the repeated fatal incidents at PT. IMIP, attributing them to a severely flawed occupational health and safety (K3) system that the management continuously neglects. There were 12 incidents of working accident in 2024, while there were already two fatalities since January this year.
"I see no concrete action from management, as workers continue to fall victims due to chaotic K3 system during production activities. What’s worse, just hours after the incident, production resumed as if nothing had happened," Komang Jordi said on Monday, February 17, 2025.
PT IMIP Media Relations Representative, Dedy Kurniawan, issued an official statement expressing regret over the incident while also criticizing the labor union’s remarks as lacking accurate information.
"We deeply regret the incident that resulted in the loss of a worker's life. However, we also regret the statements from the labor union, which were made without sufficient information about the event and were spread without a complete explanation," he said as quoted Tempo.co.id on Sunday evening, February 16, 2025.
According to eyewitness accounts, the shift change between Shift A and Shift C took place at 8 a.m. During the work briefing, two foremen provided safety instructions and assigned work areas to the workers.
At 10.30 a.m., steel slag cutting using oxy-fuel was in progress, producing many sparks. Workers in the surrounding area paused their activities to wait for the cutting process to be completed. However, Marjan Daud decided to continue working despite warnings from his colleagues to wait.
"The victim was cleaning at the end of the londer without wearing gloves. This was against the SOP, which requires workers to use assistive tools such as an iron rod or a special pulling tool. While pushing the steel slag without these tools, the victim lost balance, fell, and was crushed by the material he was pushing," Dedy said."

Source 5, Petromindo, March 29, 2025: "Qingdao Zhongzi Zhongcheng Group Co., Ltd. has announced its intention to enter into a related party transaction through its wholly-owned subsidiary, PT Transon Jasa Angkutan (TJA). The company is set to sign a "Docking and Unloading Entrustment Contract" with PT Metal Smeltindo Selaras (MSS), a company engaged in the smelting of nickel iron ore, as part of its supporting services for the Qingdao Indonesia Comprehensive Industrial Park, located in Laronae Village, Bungku Pesisir District, Morowali Regency, Central Sulawesi Province.
According to MSS Chairman Yuan Dezhiao's report on Friday (28 March), the agreement aims to provide cargo loading and unloading services for MSS's ferronickel smelting operations, which are essential to the park's operational needs. The total handling volume is expected to reach up to 4.59 million tons of bulk cargo and 12,000 tons of general cargo annually, depending on MSS’s production and procurement plans. The estimated annual service fees for the contract could amount to approximately US$9.4 million (RMB 64.98 million), although the final fee will depend on actual settlement between the two parties. MSS, which is indirectly controlled by Qingdao Urban Construction Investment (Group) Co., Ltd. (Urban Construction Group), is considered a related party of Qingdao Zhongzi Zhongcheng, making this transaction subject to disclosure and approval under the Shenzhen Stock Exchange’s regulations.
The contract will be effective from April 1, 2025, through March 31, 2028. Payment terms are in Indonesian Rupiah, with monthly settlements based on the agreed fees, calculated at a rate of $8.7 per ton for general cargo and $2.0 per ton for bulk cargo. The settlement rate will be aligned with the exchange rate announced by Bank Indonesia.
MSS's unaudited financial figures for the 2024 fiscal year and January 2025 show significant financial challenges, with operating revenue of $21.5 million (RMB 146,925,010), a total loss of $10.3 million (RMB -72,703,630), and net loss of $10.3 million (RMB -72,703,630). The company's total assets stand at $71.8 million  (RMB 495,474,110), with total liabilities at $69.6 million (RMB 480,482,770) and owner's equity at $2.2 million (RMB 14,991,350). These figures highlight MSS's ongoing financial struggles as it continues to ramp up its ferronickel production...

Source 2, Mining Technology, December 8, 2021: "Australia’s Nickel Mines has signed a binding definitive agreement with its partner Shanghai Decent Investment (Group) to acquire a 70% equity interest in the Oracle Nickel Project in Indonesia.
The $525m deal follows a memorandum of understanding (MoU) signed last month.
As agreed, Nickel Mines will purchase the majority stake in Oracle Nickel for a total consideration of $371m. The company is also obligated to invest $154m for the construction of the project through shareholder loans. The project will feature four rotary kiln electric furnace (RKEF) lines and ancillary facilities within the Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park (IMIP) in Central Sulawesi.
The RKEF lines will have a nameplate production capacity of 36,000tpa of equivalent contained nickel in nickel pig iron.
Separately, the project will build a 380MW captive power plant to support RKEF operations. Shanghai Decent will lead the design and construction of Oracle Nickel.
The four Oracle RKEF lines are expected to commence operations no later than 19 February 2023 while the power plant will be commissioned no later than 19 July 2023.
According to Nickel Mines, the overall construction costs for the project, including the power plant, will not exceed $750m. Shanghai Decent will indemnify Nickel Mines for any construction costs exceeding $750m.
Nickel Mines managing director Justin Werner said: “With our commitment to the Oracle Nickel Project coming rapidly on the heels of the fast-tracked commissioning of our 80%-owned Angel Nickel Project, Nickel Mines is set to more than triple the size of its attributable nickel production and operational cash flows over the next 15 months.
“With 12 RKEF lines in operation by Q1 2023 and approximately 100kt of attributable nickel production, Nickel Mines will sit comfortably amongst the top-ten global producers and be arguably the largest listed pureplay nickel exposure globally.”
The transaction is expected to close following a shareholder approval, which is expected to be sought during a general meeting in the first quarter of 2022."

AMI has an output capacity of 150,000 t/yr for NPI, or 24,000 t/yr of nickel metal equivalent. This acquisition will also raise Huayou's nickel matte capacity in the longer term after a technical innovation of the NPI production unit, Huayou said."

"IDX-listed coal and integrated nickel mining firm PT Harum Energy Tbk (IDX: HRUM) plans to increase its nickel ore output to 2.5 million wet metric tons (wmt) in 2025, up from 963,028 wmt in 2024. The company produces nickel ore through its indirect subsidiary PT Position, a nickel miner located in East Halmahera Regency, North Maluku Province.

PT Position holds a concession covering 4,017 hectares with 215 million dry metric tons of nickel resources and began production in October 2024.
In the nickel downstream segment, Harum produces nickel pig iron (NPI) and high-grade nickel matte through its smelters, PT Infei Metal Industry (IMI) and PT Westrong Metal Industry (WMI), both located in Weda Bay Industrial Park, Central Halmahera. The company targets production of 71,000 to 75,000 tons of nickel (NPI and high-grade matte) this year, up from 57,583 tons in 2024. Harum also plans to commence production of mixed-hydroxide precipitate (MHP) from its HPAL smelter, PT Blue Sparking Energy (BSE), by the end of 2025.

Source 4, Indonesia Miner, April 22, 2024:
"PT Harum Energy Tbk (HRUM) has entered into a strategic partnership to develop its nickel portfolio as part of its business diversification strategy. The company has signed a memorandum of understanding with a Chinese company, Eternal Tsingshan Group Limited (ET) as a strategic partner. The signing was carried out on April 5, 2024.
Quoting the disclosure of information from the Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX), most of HRUM's nickel business portfolio is under controlled companies, namely PT Harum Nickel Perkasa (HNP) and PT Tanito Harum Nickel (THN), which include majority ownership in PT Position, PT Infei Metal Industry, PT Westrong Metal Industry, PT Blue Sparking Energy, and minority ownership in PT Sunny Metal Industry.
ET is a company incorporated in Hong Kong SAR and, through its affiliates, has invested extensively in Indonesia, primarily in nickel processing and refining activities (Mitra Group). The Mitra Group is also the operating partner of the company's smelter project and the manager of the Weda Bay Industrial Estate in North Maluku Province, where the projects are located.
To realize the strategic partnership, the company's subsidiaries, HNP and THN, intend to initially issue mandatory convertible debentures (SUWK) which will be subscribed by the strategic partner either directly or through one or more companies to be determined later.
The SUWK will be converted into new shares in HNP and THN representing an effective shareholding of up to 49% in the Company's Nickel Portfolio, whereby the terms including the principal amount, maturity date, and conversion period of the SUWK will be agreed upon by the parties, subject to applicable regulations.
Following the conversion of the SUWK, the Company remains the effective majority shareholder in the Company's Nickel Portfolio.
“The company and its subsidiaries will use the proceeds obtained from taking part in the SUWK to settle some of the obligations relating to some assets acquisition in the Company's Nickel Portfolio," wrote the company management on Thursday (18/4).
Both parties will then prepare the documentation and all requirements to realize the strategic cooperation and the issuance of the SUWK following applicable regulations.
"The Company and Strategic Partner are targeting that the SUWK can be issued in the third quarter of 2024," he said.
The strategic cooperation planned in the memorandum of understanding is expected to bring benefits and create various business opportunities for the company, including obtaining synergies from the combined experience and capabilities of the company and partner groups in the mining and processing of nickel products to maximize the efficiency and operational performance of the company's nickel portfolio going forward.
More benefits for the company would be to ensure the sustainability of the management and operation of projects in the company's nickel portfolio through strategic cooperation between the company and the partner group.
In addition, to improve the capital structure of the company's nickel portfolio through a decrease in the ratio of liabilities to equity after the SUWK conversion implementation. Then, forming an integrated nickel industry portfolio from upstream to downstream with diverse nickel products, to increase the Company's ability to raise funds to finance further business development.
Furthermore, the benefits are to support technology transfer efforts in the nickel refining and processing industry, support knowledge transfer efforts in marketing activities, and access to the supply chain of the stainless steel and battery industry in the global market.
The company believes that the signing of the memorandum of understanding has no material negative impact on the operational, legal, financial, or business continuity.
"There is no affiliation between ET and the company, HNP and THN. Apart from the memorandum of understanding signed by the company and ET, there are no other definitive documents signed in connection with the strategic cooperation, therefore there have been no transactions carried out by the Company or companies controlled by the company as a realization of strategic cooperation," he concluded."

Source 2, Fajar Malut, March 6, 2022: "The issuance of a permit to operate a power plant at PT Jiu Long Metal Industri in the mining business permit area of ​​PT. IWIP issued byInvestment and One-Stop Integrated Services Agency (DPMPTSP) of North Maluku Province ( Malut ), was deemed to be procedurally flawed without a technical review. 
North Maluku Electricity Observer, M. Apriyadi said, the issuance of the electricity supply business permit (IUPTLS) through the decision of the Head of the North Maluku DPMPTSP Service, Bambang Hermawan with Number: 502/12/DPMPTSP/IOPTL/2020, issued on November 26, 2020 regarding the electricity generator operating permit to PT Jou Long Metal Industri is procedurally flawed.
Apriyadi continued, the coal-fired steam power plant with a capacity of 3×380 MW, equipped with the main equipment of 3 boiler units, 3 turbine units, 3 generator units and supporting equipment, is suspected of having been operating without a technical study and is not equipped with an Operational Feasibility Certificate (SLO) document.
"The 12 diesel-fueled power plants are suspected of operating without a technical study and without a SLO document," Apriyadi told reporters on Sunday (March 6, 2022).
He added that because the permit was issued without any administrative process, the technical team from the Electricity Division of the North Maluku DPMPTSP Service did not receive the application documents. Therefore, they did not conduct the technical inspection and verification stages of the application documents.
"Because the application documents were not subjected to technical inspection and verification, the technical team failed to provide a technical basis for issuing the IUPTLS. Therefore, the permit is procedurally flawed and invalid. Therefore, even if the power plant's operational feasibility test has been conducted, the SLO will certainly be invalid," Apriyadi explained.
Apriyadi stated that such work violates Article 8 and is therefore subject to Article 103 of the Regulation of the Minister of Energy and Mineral Resources of the Republic of Indonesia Number 11 of 2021 concerning the implementation of electricity business.
"Permits in the electricity sector are considered high-risk, so they must undergo a thorough technical inspection and verification process in the field to avoid unwanted technical errors. This permit from the North Maluku DPMPTSP must be revoked, and sanctions imposed in accordance with applicable regulations," he stressed.
Separately, the Head of the Malut DPMPTSP Service, Bambang Hermawan, when confirmed by telephone, said that his party issued the permit in accordance with the technical study and the Certificate of Operational Eligibility (SLO) document as well as the permit for functional feasibility testing.
"It is impossible for us to issue a permit without going through a technical study and accompanied by documents and a functional testing permit," he said."

Source 3, Tegas, August 2, 2021: "Former Director of General Crimes of the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police, Senior Commissioner of Police La Ode Aries El-Fathar, was reported to the Propam of the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police on Monday (19/7/2021). The report filed by PT MBG Nikel Indonesia against the former Director of General Crimes was not without reason. According to MBG, La Ode is suspected of being involved in backing a mining mafia that embezzled and exploited land above the IUP of PT MBG Nikel Indonesia.
The Head of the MBG Nickel Case Analysis Team, Deny Lee, explained that the land management incident occurred around 1 year ago, since the PT MBG land was involved in a case with Martinus Roga, then his team heard about land management on the disputed land.
"As a result of the investigation, our team obtained documentary evidence of land management on disputed land whose case was currently being processed by the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police, La Ode Aries El-Fathar, who at that time served as the Director of Criminal Investigation of the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police, immediately summoned Hirjan, S.Si as the Attorney of the Director of PT. MBG Nikel Indonesia to meet with the Joint Operational (JO) land processor, namely PT. Jaya Bersama Sahabat (JBS) who had entered into a cooperation contract with Martinus Roga," Deny explained.
In a meeting at the residence of La Ode Aries El-Fathar in the Citra Land Kendari Ruko Housing Complex, it was revealed that the President Director of JBS, La Ode Sahrulan, is a close relative of the wife of the former Director of Criminal Investigation of the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police.
"But it's not a problem for us who was involved, what is clear is that he made a land management agreement with the person on the disputed land whose case he is currently handling," said Deny.
"We took many photos of the processing, including the construction of buildings, and of course, this is something that has been going on for quite some time for them to cultivate land on disputed land," he continued.
Deny admitted that the mining was carried out under the name of PT Wisnu Mandiri Batara, which is part of PT MBG Nikel. However, according to Deny, this was all done through fraud and falsified deeds.
"That's why we were shocked, so we filed a report regarding the deed owned by Martinus Roga, which we strongly suspect he obtained through illegal means," said Deny.
Deny said that based on the report, processing should have been halted to prevent further losses. On the one hand, La Ode Aries ordered Hirjan to forcibly open the MBG Nikel office in the Citra Land area and take control. However, he also ignored Hirjan's request to install police tape around the processing site, citing legal grounds to prevent further losses.
"So who's responsible now? If we're going through the legal process while the case is still being processed there? That's strange. How is the legal process going to work?" he asked, puzzled..."


Source 2, Tegas, May 16, 2021: "South Korean mining company Made By Good (MBG) Group, through its subsidiary PT MBG Nikel Indonesia, has begun construction of a nickel smelter in North Konawe Regency (Konut), Southeast Sulawesi. The project's investment value is estimated at Rp76 trillion.
"We appreciate the North Konawe Regency government for its role in attracting investors from the industrial sector," said the Director of Industrial Area Development I of the Ministry of Industry, Arus Gunawan in Jakarta, Wednesday (17/01/2018) as quoted from a press release from the Ministry of Industry of the Republic of Indonesia.
Arus said that the company had laid the first stone on January 2, 2018. On that occasion, Arus was also present to witness the process together with the Regent of Konut Ruksamin, President Director of MBG Group Lim Dong Pyo and Commissioner of PT MBG Nikel Indonesia Jang Jongsoo.
The nickel smelter is planned to be completed in stages over the next two years and is expected to employ thousands of workers. Furthermore, the land area used will be approximately 311 hectares.
However, as time went by, the construction of this management plant experienced prolonged obstacles until the 2021 period, when the police temporarily stopped the construction due to several cases that had not yet been resolved.
Report of falsification of Factory Building Permit (IMB)
Following up on the problem of factory construction that has not yet progressed, the Director's Attorney, Hirjan, S.Si, when met by the media crew, explained that this problem started with Han Soonju's report against Yi Chang Keong for alleged falsification of the factory's IMB.
"The report was submitted in January 2020 and has been withdrawn according to statements from the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police, in this case IPDA Abdul Rizal SH and Bripka Muhammad Akbar as investigators handling the case," explained Hirjan.
"Even if the complaint is withdrawn, the legal process should continue because this is not a family complaint offense, but rather a common offense because they are not related, so there could be some truth to this case," Hirjan added.
Hirjan further explained that when Han Soonju received a report regarding the forgery of the factory's building permit, he, representing the Board of Directors, also reported Han Soonju to the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police regarding the forgery of company documents and embezzlement with losses estimated at 10 billion.
Document Forgery Report
Apart from Han Soonju's report regarding the factory's IMB, the Director's Attorney, Hirjan, revealed that his party had also reported Han Soonju on suspicion of document falsification and embezzlement of company assets with the victim being Lim Dong Pyo, President Director of MBG Group.
"Han Soonju falsified the company's deed documents without the knowledge of the other shareholders," explained Hirjan.
"In the fake document, Han Soonju served as President Director of MBG Group, which should have been filled by Lim Dong Pyo," he continued.
The forgery of this deed, he said, was considered odd and legally flawed because one of the formal requirements was not fulfilled so that the notary where the Deed was issued issued a letter of delivery and clarification regarding the cancellation of the deed he had issued, where in the process of releasing shares, Yi Chang Keong, the owner of 5% of the shares in the circular letter replacing the GMS of PT. MBG Nikel Indonesia, did not receive the money from the release of his shares, also the owner of the largest share of MBG INC was also known to be absent and did not agree to the signing of the circular letter.
"Therefore, it is illogical that the deed exists and is issued without the knowledge and approval of the majority shareholder," he stressed.
He added that the report was filed on March 27, 2020, but to date, there has been no progress from the investigator (Bripka Muammar Rajiv) from the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police's Criminal Investigation Directorate. Even more curiously, he continued, the report was dismissed on March 9, 2021, on the grounds that it did not constitute a crime.
"I don't see any motivation from the authorities to resolve this case, therefore I suspect there are conditions of individual interests in this case, if the process of resolving a problem takes a long time and is difficult to resolve, it means that the person resolving the problem also "gets" a share of the problem," he said.
"As of May 2021, there has been no progress whatsoever in this case, so construction at PT. MBG Nikel is also not proceeding as planned," Hirjan explained to the media crew.
He continued that after Eid, his party would report to the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police Inspectorate to resolve this long-standing problem.
"Hopefully, this problem can be handled quickly so that factory construction can continue," he concluded.
Clarification from the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police
Responding to accusations from the Attorney General of PT. MBG Nikel Indonesia, Hijran, S.Si., regarding the stalled development of the case being handled by investigators at the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police's Criminal Investigation Directorate, media crews attempted to contact Sub-Directorate 3 of the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police's Criminal Investigation Directorate directly. However, investigators advised them to contact the Unit Head directly for clearer information.
"Regarding this case, you can ask the Regional Police Public Relations directly," said Unit Head Abdul Rizal when contacted by telephone.
In a different place, the Head of the Public Information Sub-Division of the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police, Commissioner Dolfi Kumaseh, explained that after his party confirmed with the Southeast Sulawesi Regional Police's Criminal Investigation Directorate regarding the stalled investigation into the forgery and embezzlement case with No. LP 150, Dolfi stated that the case had been SP3.
"The investigation into LP 150 has been terminated. A decision has been made regarding the investigation, stating that it is not a criminal offense but rather a civil matter," Dolfi explained (May 11, 2021)..."

Source 3, SMM, September 20, 2023:
"On August 28, 2023, in the smelting workshop of Indonesia Hengsheng New Energy Materials Co., Ltd., as the fiery red low nickel matte slowly flowed out, it marked that the world's first oxygen-enriched side-blown process to process laterite nickel ore to produce matte nickel production line was successfully put into operation.
Indonesia Hengsheng New Energy Materials Co., Ltd. is jointly funded and constructed by Hainan Riseco, Indonesia Huadi, and Hong Kong Zhongwei. It is located in Huadi Industrial Park, BANTAENG County, South Sulawesi Province, Indonesia. It mainly engages in laterite nickel Ore smelting and processing business, the first phase construction project with an annual output of 10,000 tons of nickel metal equivalent (annual processing of approximately 1.1 million tons of laterite nickel ore). The planned land area of Huadi Industrial Park is 400 hectares, which is adjacent to the seaside road and has a self-built barge terminal.
On August 28, 2023, in the smelting workshop of Indonesia Hengsheng New Energy Materials Co., Ltd., as the fiery red low nickel matte slowly flowed out, it marked that the world's first oxygen-enriched side-blown process to process laterite nickel ore to produce matte nickel production line was successfully put into operation.
Indonesia Hengsheng New Energy Materials Co., Ltd. is jointly funded and constructed by Hainan Riseko, Indonesia Huadi, Hong Kong Zhongwei, and Shengwei New Energy. The company is located in Huadi Industry, BANTAENG County, South Sulawesi Province, Indonesia. The park is mainly engaged in the smelting and processing business of laterite nickel ore. The first phase of the project is to build a low matte nickel project with an annual output of 10,000 tons of nickel metal equivalent.
Hainan Riseco New Energy Co., Ltd., the controlling shareholder of Indonesian Hengsheng, is an emerging growth enterprise focusing on the development and recycling of nickel and cobalt resources and the development and application of advanced nickel and cobalt smelting technology. It adheres to the corporate development philosophy of innovation, excellence, environmental protection, and win-win. , committed to seeking greener, more cost-effective and more cost-effective solutions for the nickel and cobalt industry. The company uses Hainan as its base to radiate to other nickel and cobalt resource countries. The first cooperation demonstration project started construction in Huadi Industrial Park in Indonesia. It will continue to expand to other countries or regions and gradually develop into a world-class and industry-leading nickel and cobalt resource and Technology Provider.
The oxygen-enriched side-blown furnace process used by Indonesia's Hengsheng is the industry's first advanced smelting technology applied to laterite nickel ore smelting. This technology pre-treats laterite nickel ore through drying kilns and rotary kilns, and then sends it to an oxygen-enriched side-blown furnace for smelting to produce nickel matte. Compared with traditional nickel smelting processes such as RKEF and HPAL, this process has the following technical characteristics:
1. Highly efficient and energy-saving, reducing carbon emissions. This process uses oxygen-enriched smelting technology during the production process, which is highly efficient and energy-saving. By allowing the fuel to fully react in an oxygen-rich environment, this process reduces carbon emissions produced by incomplete combustion, reduces the use of coal and coke, and also greatly reduces the demand for secondary energy electricity and reduces carbon dioxide emissions. This process achieves efficient use of fuel, reduces energy waste, and lowers production costs. This not only helps reduce global carbon emissions, but also promotes sustainable development. In the future, the process will be further optimized, such as using natural gas to replace coal, using hydrogen to replace natural gas, using photovoltaic power generation to supplement power demand, etc., to further reduce carbon emissions and promote the Indonesian nickel industry to continue to approach and achieve clean nickel production.
2. High comprehensive utilization rate of resources.
This process has excellent performance in comprehensive utilization of resources. First of all, its flexibility allows it to effectively utilize high-grade and low-grade nickel ores, which means that we can mine nickel resources more widely, not only able to meet the needs of the high-grade ore market, but also effectively develop low-grade ores , improve the comprehensive utilization of resources. This helps ensure our nickel supply chain is more stable and is not affected by fluctuations in ore grades. In addition, the process includes a cobalt recovery process. Cobalt is a key strategic metal widely used in batteries, aerospace and medical devices. By recycling cobalt, we not only reduce the demand for finite cobalt resources, but also reduce the environmental pressure of cobalt mining. This further improves the sustainability and comprehensive utilization of resources and is in line with the principles of sustainable development.
3. Environmentally friendly, the waste residue can be comprehensively utilized.
This process also shows significant advantages in environmental protection. Through the high-temperature smelting process, the properties of the waste residue become very stable and there is no harm to the environment. This means we can effectively handle and dispose of waste residues without worrying about pollution or resource waste. What’s even more encouraging is that these waste residues are not only harmless, but can also be further comprehensively utilized. Waste residue can be processed into building materials such as cement, which helps reduce the need for virgin building materials and lowers the environmental impact of mining and manufacturing. This recycling of resources further highlights the sustainability of the process, converting waste residue into useful products, reducing waste emissions and resource waste..."

Source 6, Harian Ekonomi Neraca, October 13, 2025: "ANNOUNCEMENT OF SUMMARY OF THE MERGER PLAN OF
PT JADE BAY METAL INDUSTRY
INTO PT DEBONAIR NICKEL INDONESIA
I. Brief Description of the Merger Participating Companies
In connection with the planned merger of PT Jade Bay Metal Industry (hereinafter referred to as JBM I) into
PT Debonair Nickel Indonesia (hereinafter referred to as DNI) (hereinafter referred to as JBMI and DNI as the
Merger Participating Companies), and in order to comply with the merger requirements of Law No. 40 of 2007 concerning Limited Liability Companies and its implementing regulations, as well as the articles of association of the Merger Participating Companies, the Board of Directors of the Merger Participating Companies hereby announces a summary of the merger plan as follows:
A. JBM I
JBMI is a limited liability company established under the laws and regulations of the Republic of Indonesia and domiciled in South Jakarta. JBMI operates in the Non-Ferrous Base Metal Manufacturing Industry.
B. DNI
DNI is a limited liability company established under the laws and regulations of the Republic of Indonesia and domiciled in South Jakarta. DNI operates in the Non-Ferrous Base Metal Manufacturing Industry.
PT DNI and PT JMI are two companies engaged in the non-ferrous base metal processing industry and both own and operate smelter facilities (processing and refining nickel ore).
In practice, both companies have nearly identical business lines and production processes, with operational locations located adjacent to or integrated within a single industrial area. The proposed merger between DNI and JMI has significant operational and financial strategic benefits and provides an opportunity to create significant value for both companies, their respective shareholders, and all stakeholders involved. This merger will create a surviving company with a larger scale and a more efficient cost structure, enabling it to accelerate investment and increase production.
The merger of these smelter companies aims to form a stronger, more efficient, and more competitive entity.
Through the consolidation of resources, technology, and operational capacity, the merged company will be able to achieve higher production efficiency, strengthen its financial structure, and increase its competitiveness in the nickel industry. Furthermore, this merger aligns with government policies regarding mineral downstreaming and increasing domestic added value, while also supporting long-term growth, business sustainability, and compliance with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards.
III. Share Valuation and Conversion Procedures
The procedures for the valuation and conversion of shares of the Merger-Participating Companies are conducted in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
IV. Important Provisions of the Merger
1. The proposed merger will become effective upon the signing of the Deed of Merger and the issuance of approval from the Minister of Law and Human Rights regarding the amendment to DNI's articles of association in connection with the Merger (hereinafter referred to as the Effective Date).
2. As a result of the Merger, DNI will become the surviving company, and JBMI will be dissolved by operation of law without liquidation. Therefore, as of the Effective Date, all assets, liabilities, employees, rights, and obligations to third parties of JBMI will be transferred by operation of law to DNI. 3. All third parties, including but not limited to creditors and each participating company in the merger, are given 14 (fourteen) calendar days from the date of this announcement to submit written objections, accompanied by reasons and supporting evidence, to each company at the addresses listed below.
4. If within the aforementioned time period, the third party does not submit any objections, then the third party is deemed to have approved the merger.
5. DNI, as the surviving company, hereby confirms that DNI will take over and assume all assets, liabilities, rights, obligations, business operations, and employees of JBMI as a result of the merger.
V. Additional Information
For creditors and third parties who require further information regarding this Merger, you can
contact each foreign company using the information mentioned below:
PT DEBONAIR NICKEL INDONESIA PT JADE BAY METAL INDUSTRY
S o podel O ficer T ow er A la n ta i 21
Jl. WEST MEGA WINNING III, Lot 10, EAST WINNING, STIBU D
South Jakarta 021 -5 0 8 0 6 5 8 6

PT DNI and PT JMI are two companies engaged in the non-ferrous base metal processing industry and both own and operate smelter facilities (processing and refining nickel ore).

The proposed merger between DNI and JMI has significant operational and financial strategic benefits and provides an opportunity to create significant value for both companies, their respective shareholders, and all stakeholders involved. This merger will create a surviving company with a larger scale and a more efficient cost structure, enabling it to accelerate investment and increase production.
The merger of these smelter companies aims to form a stronger, more efficient, and more competitive entity.
Through the consolidation of resources, technology, and operational capacity, the merged company will be able to achieve higher production efficiency, strengthen its financial structure, and increase its competitiveness in the nickel industry. Furthermore, this merger aligns with government policies regarding mineral downstreaming and increasing domestic added value, while also supporting long-term growth, business sustainability, and compliance with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards.
III. Share Valuation and Conversion Procedures
The procedures for the valuation and conversion of shares of the Merger-Participating Companies are conducted in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.
IV. Important Provisions of the Merger
1. The proposed merger will become effective upon the signing of the Deed of Merger and the issuance of approval from the Minister of Law and Human Rights regarding the amendment to DNI's articles of association in connection with the Merger (hereinafter referred to as the Effective Date).
2. As a result of the Merger, DNI will become the surviving company, and JBMI will be dissolved by operation of law without liquidation. Therefore, as of the Effective Date, all assets, liabilities, employees, rights, and obligations to third parties of JBMI will be transferred by operation of law to DNI. 3. All third parties, including but not limited to creditors and each participating company in the merger, are given 14 (fourteen) calendar days from the date of this announcement to submit written objections, accompanied by reasons and supporting evidence, to each company at the addresses listed below.
4. If within the aforementioned time period, the third party does not submit any objections, then the third party is deemed to have approved the merger.
5. DNI, as the surviving company, hereby confirms that DNI will take over and assume all assets, liabilities, rights, obligations, business operations, and employees of JBMI as a result of the merger.

Source 4, CNGR 2024 Sustainability Report, April 10, 2025: 
CNGR engaged independent third-party organizations to conduct mineral supply chain due diligence audits. In 2024, the Tongren Industrial Base, Ningxiang Industrial Base, and Qinzhou Industrial Base participated in either initial assessments or reassessments under the Responsible Minerals Initiative's Responsible Minerals Assurance Process (RMI RMAP), covering both nickel and cobalt. The Zhongtsing Project at the Morowali Industrial Base and the Debonair Project at the WedaBay Industrial Base successfully passed the Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) audits under the Responsible Minerals Initiative (RMI), becoming the first two companies in Indonesia to complete both RMAP and ESG audits. This achievement not only represents a high-level recognition of CNGR's efforts in supply chain due diligence management, but also sets a new benchmark for the regulated development of the new energy industry. The RMI RMAP is a responsible mineral supply chain certification program that audits and certifies smelters and refiners to ensure their mineral sourcing meets ethical and sustainable standards, particularly avoiding conflict minerals. ESG audits provide a comprehensive evaluation of a company's
performance in environmental protection, occupational health and safety, social responsibility, and corporate governance. The successful completion of these audits by the Zhongtsing and Debonair Projects demonstrates CNGR's commitment to aligning its responsible mineral supply chain management and ESG performance with internationally recognized high standards, and highlights the significant progress achieved in this area.
The company requires key smelters within the supply chain to undergo independent third-party audits. As of now, 90% of domestic and international cobalt suppliers have passed third-party audits under the Responsible Minerals Initiative (RMI); for nickel suppliers, 50% have passed RMI third-party audits, representing a significant increase compared to the previous year. The company encourages more suppliers utilizing recycled materials to undergo independent third party audits."
